---
title: "Reading roadmap"
description: "Ways through the book, chapter maturity, and just-in-time supporting material."
page-role: frontmatter
---

# Reading roadmap {.unnumbered}

The default route follows the parts in order. It is the safest route for a new
reader because later chapters assume the measurement habits, reference
directions, model limits, and evidence practices established in Part I. A route
is a way to emphasize a destination, not permission to ignore prerequisites.

## Choose a destination

| Destination | Begin with | Then emphasize | Demonstrate readiness with |
|---|---|---|---|
| First trustworthy bench investigation | [safe practice](01-foundations/f01-safe-practice.qmd) | F02–F06 | a prediction, current-limited test, uncertainty statement, and bounded conclusion |
| Analog instrument | Part I | A01–A06, S01–S02, R05 | a calibrated signal chain with an error budget |
| Digital and embedded system | F01–F07, A01–A02 | D01–D05, E01–E05 | timed interface evidence, fault recovery, and reproducible firmware |
| Custom electronic product | Parts I–IV | R01–R06 alongside the relevant domain chapters | a reviewed design baseline, staged bring-up, verification matrix, and release package |
| Complete field system | the full route or equivalent demonstrated knowledge | X01–X05 | qualification evidence that another person can reproduce and maintain |

Institutional course mappings provide paced routes through the same chapter
IDs. The table above is for readers selecting a destination rather than a
semester.

## Use the appendices just in time

The appendices are working references, not a final hurdle. Follow a prerequisite
link to them when a chapter first needs the technique:

- M01 for units, graphs, algebra, and complex quantities;
- M02 for dynamic models, differential equations, and linear algebra;
- M03 for uncertainty, statistics, and numerical decisions;
- T01 for datasheets, standards, and source quality;
- T02 for tools, automation, files, and version control;
- T03 for records that another person can reproduce and audit.

## Practice and project spine

Book chapters develop durable models. Labs ask for physical or authentic
evidence; projects force several models and interfaces to succeed together.
Their stable IDs allow a class or independent implementation to change
apparatus without copying the theory.

| Stage | Evidence sequence | Integrating result |
|---|---|---|
| Foundations | L01–L05: safe instruments, loaded networks, power, transients, and frequency response | M1: a qualified energy-and-light measurement claim |
| Devices and computation | L06–L10: device regions, analog limits, calibration, timing, HDL, and FPGA evidence | M2–M3: an environmental monitor and precision instrument |
| Embedded domains | L11–L18: drivers, protocols, control, power, mixed signal, radio, and recovery | M4–M6: a data logger, secure node, and field pilot |
| Realization and handover | L19–L20: repeat-build, production-test, and field-readiness evidence | M7–M8: risk retirement, qualification, and reproducible handover |

The lab IDs denote evidence goals, not permission to perform an activity. The
safety level, apparatus, stop conditions, and local authorization must be
defined before a lab becomes deliverable.

## Interpret chapter status

| Status | What the reader can rely on |
|---|---|
| `outline` | scope, central question, concept sequence, and dependency boundary |
| `draft` | complete instructional path, still subject to technical and editorial revision |
| `review` | worked results and evidence checked; review findings may still change the chapter |
| `released` | accepted for the stated edition and still bounded by its documented assumptions |

Status describes maturity, not importance. Regardless of status, prose in this
book does not authorize work beyond the stated safety boundary.

## A productive chapter cycle

Before reading, state a directional prediction and the evidence that might
change your mind. While reading, track the physical system, schematic or
architecture, equations, assumptions, and test points as one model. At the end,
recompute the central result, explain a plausible mismatch, and state the claim
that the available evidence supports—no stronger.
